ARM.thesis: ARM's outperformance during broad semiconductor selloff indicates flight to quality in CPU/GPU IP licensing; earnings visibility and foundational position in AI chip architecture should support continued upside as market rotates back to quality growth.

MRVL.thesis: MRVL benefits from secular AI capex cycle and data center networking TAM expansion. Outperformance during correction signals institutional quality rotation; breaking above recent highs would confirm continuation. Catalysts: upcoming earnings guidance on AI/cloud adoption trends, semiconductor demand data.

NVDA.thesis: NVIDIA benefits from AI capex cycle durability (SoftBank/OpenAI $852B valuation validates infrastructure spending) and geopolitical de-escalation reducing macro uncertainty.

MU.thesis:
Micron reported earnings beat confirming HBM/AI memory supercycle reacceleration with massive revenue growth; stock surging on volume.
• Momentum: +9% today after earnings; vs SMH -1.8% 1W — massive relative strength
• Technicals: Breakout on earnings catalyst; ATR 28.7, needs room; -21.9% from 52W high means still recovery mode
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 3.7x on 196% revenue growth with 41% margins — deeply undervalued on forward basis
• Catalyst: Q2 earnings massive beat today; HBM memory demand from AI data centers reaccelerating
• Volume: Vol ratio 1.49x avg, today likely 3-5x on earnings — institutional accumulation
• Sizing: 2.9% weight — moderate size given post-earnings volatility risk (gap fade possible) but fundamentals too compelling to ignore

TSM.thesis:
TSM is the foundry infrastructure backbone for all AI chip manufacturing; reporting April 16 with strong setup as AI capex cycle continues.
• Momentum: -1.8% 1W, -3% 1M — lagging but bottoming relative to AI names; vol ratio 1.03x steady
• Technicals: -12.2% from 52W high — recovery opportunity; support confirmed at current levels
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 19x, 20% revenue growth, 45% net margins, 35% ROE — best-in-class foundry economics
• Catalyst: Earnings April 16 — AI chip demand commentary will be market-moving; NVIDIA/AMD/MRVL all ramping orders
• Volume: Vol ratio 1.03x — neutral, no distribution signal
• Sizing: 2.7% weight — pre-earnings positioning sized conservatively given Taiwan geopolitical risk premium

NEM.thesis:
NEM is the highest-quality gold miner offering leveraged exposure to gold's rate-cut driven rally with defensive characteristics.
• Momentum: +12.1% 1W — massive outperformance vs SPY, QQQ, SMH; strongest performer on watchlist
• Technicals: Strong uptrend breaking out; -15.5% from 52W high means room to run; ATR 5.2
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 10.5x on 21% revenue growth, 31% margins, 22% ROE — value in commodity cycle
• Catalyst: Gold surging on Fed rate-cut bets + Iran resolution optimism reducing oil but increasing safe haven; Bloomberg reports gold extending gains
• Volume: 0.93x — modest, not extended on volume yet
• Sizing: 1.7% weight — tactical diversifier/hedge; not a core thesis but adds portfolio balance against semiconductor concentration

GS.thesis:
GS positioned to benefit from risk-on environment and capital markets reactivation ahead of April 13 earnings; sector diversifier from semiconductor concentration.
• Momentum: +2.2% 1W, -0.3% 1M — modest outperformance vs SPY; banking peers outperforming
• Technicals: -12.2% from 52W high — recovery opportunity; consolidating above support; ATR 24.9
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 13.2x on 15% revenue growth, 29% margins — reasonable valuation for premier IB
• Catalyst: Earnings April 13; M&A/IPO pipeline reactivating (SpaceX IPO filing, Oracle $16B financing) drives fees; rate-cut bets support trading revenues
• Volume: 0.69x — low, pre-earnings positioning opportunity before institutional accumulation
• Sizing: 3.4% weight — solid conviction pre-earnings play; sized to reflect meaningful but not outsized positioning given earnings binary risk

SNDK.thesis:
SNDK is the pure-play AI storage beneficiary from Western Digital's spin-off, trading at a deep discount to memory peers on a forward P/E basis while participating in the AI data center storage supercycle.
• Momentum: +16.3% 1W vs SMH +3.0% — massive relative outperformance; +17.1% 1M showing sustained institutional accumulation
• Technicals: -9.8% from 52W high — meaningful room to recover; ATR 62, high volatility but strong uptrend; vol ratio 0.91x (not overbought on volume)
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 8.0x on +61% revenue growth — one of the cheapest growth profiles in the universe; 36% margins; D/E 7.96 manageable
• Catalyst: Jim Cramer called memory rivals a 'Gold Mine' amid AI storage crunch; WDC valuation piece noting AI storage optimism; MU's earnings beat validating memory supercycle which reads across to SNDK
• Volume: 0.91x 20d avg — steady accumulation, not speculative spike; confirms institutional rather than retail-driven move
• Sizing: 3.5% portfolio weight, max ~$1,050 loss if 6% stop hit, appropriate for moderate-high conviction speculative position in high-beta memory name

AMD.thesis:
AMD is a primary beneficiary of AI compute diversification away from NVIDIA monopoly, with Citi placing it on 30-day positive catalyst watch alongside ADI — fresh institutional momentum signal heading into earnings season.
• Momentum: +9.0% 1W vs SPY +3.9%, QQQ +4.6%, SMH +5.8% — strongly outperforming; +10.4% 1M confirming sustained accumulation
• Technicals: -17.6% from 52W high — meaningful recovery room; vol ratio 0.90x near normal (not over-extended); ATR 10.4 manageable; breaking out above recent resistance
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 20.4x on +34% revenue growth, 13% margins improving; D/E 6.36x conservative; ROE 7% but accelerating
• Catalyst: Citi 30-day positive catalyst watch (2h ago) — specific near-term signal; hyperscaler AI chip demand diversification theme gaining traction; AMD data center GPU ramp ongoing
• Volume: Vol ratio 0.90x — steady institutional accumulation without over-extension; confirms orderly breakout
• Sizing: 3.3% weight at 15 shares; not larger because semi sector already ~35% of portfolio; not smaller because Citi catalyst watch + momentum justify meaningful position

WDC.thesis:
WDC is the parent/partner to SNDK in the AI storage supercycle, benefiting from HDD/flash demand surge in AI data centers with fresh momentum catalyst today.
• Momentum: +10.5% 1W vs SMH +5.8% — significant outperformance; +17.4% 1M showing sustained institutional AI storage narrative; fresh news today 'Why Western Digital Stock Is Up Today'
• Technicals: -4.8% from 52W high — near all-time highs with room; ATR 24.6 shows volatility but manageable at 5-share size; vol ratio 0.86x healthy
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 22.3x on +25% revenue growth, 36% margins, ROE 41% — quality metrics improving sharply
• Catalyst: AI data center storage supercycle driving enterprise HDD and flash demand; SNDK spin-off re-rating WDC valuation; 'Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup' coverage today
• Volume: Vol ratio 0.86x — above average confirming institutional buying into storage thesis
• Sizing: 1.5% weight; small because SNDK already covers AI storage primary; WDC is complementary second exposure; not zero because momentum and fundamentals both compelling

ANET.thesis:
ANET is the backbone AI data center networking infrastructure play with exceptional fundamentals and accelerating institutional demand.
• Momentum: +18.1% 1W vs SPY +4.0%, QQQ +5.0%, SMH +10.3% — dramatically outperforming all benchmarks; +5.8% 1M showing sustained accumulation
• Technicals: -12.0% from 52W high — meaningful recovery potential; vol ratio 1.87x shows institutional buying; ATR 6.82 manageable
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 33.9x on 29% revenue growth, 39% net margins, D/E 0.73 (pristine balance sheet), ROE 31% — premium justified
• Catalyst: Iran cease-fire accelerates AI infrastructure build-out (removes uncertainty); BofA $1.3T chip forecast drives hyperscaler capex; Zacks estimate revision uptrend
• Volume: Vol ratio 1.87x (highest in watchlist alongside MPWR) — strong institutional confirmation of breakout
• Sizing: 2.9% weight appropriate for new position; not larger because already have significant semi/AI exposure (NVDA, MRVL, AMD, MU); not smaller because momentum and institutional signal are compelling

AVGO.thesis:
AVGO is named by BofA as a top AI chip driver in the $1.3T 2026 semiconductor forecast, with exceptional fundamentals and undervaluation relative to peers.
• Momentum: +13.3% 1W vs SMH +10.3% — outperforming even the red-hot semiconductor ETF; vol ratio 1.22x confirms institutional support
• Technicals: -15.1% from 52W high — meaningful recovery room; near-term breakout pattern; ATR 12.66
• Fundamentals: Fwd P/E 19.7x on 30% revenue growth, 37% net margins — best valuation/growth combo in large-cap semis; ROE 33%
• Catalyst: BofA $1.3T chip forecast names AVGO as top driver; Iran cease-fire accelerates AI infrastructure spend; IBD new buy signal today
• Volume: Vol ratio 1.22x — steady institutional accumulation confirming momentum
• Sizing: 2.8% weight; not larger because semiconductor concentration already high; not smaller because BofA specific namecheck + valuation + momentum create triple confirmation

ORCL.thesis: Oracle announced it is buying fuel cell power from Bloom Energy for data centers — a direct play on the AI data center power infrastructure theme. This drove a significant move with 2.33x vol ratio (highest institutional conviction signal in the watchlist). ORCL is also one of the most beaten down mega-cap tech names at -52.5% from 52W high, creating asymmetric recovery potential. Momentum: +14.2% 1W vs QQQ +6.8% — strong outperformance on fresh catalyst. Technicals: -52.5% from 52W high is extreme — either value trap or massive recovery opportunity; the Bloom Energy deal provides a specific fundamental catalyst for the power narrative. Fundamentals: fwd P/E 20.4x on 22% revenue growth with 25% margins and 58% ROE — excellent fundamentals for the price. Catalyst: Bloom Energy fuel cell power deal for AI data centers announced this week is the specific trigger; cloud/AI infrastructure buildout thesis. Volume: 2.33x vol ratio is the highest in the watchlist — strong institutional conviction signal confirming the move. Sizing: 1.1% initial position — small due to the recovery-from-deep-drawdown risk profile; will size up after confirming momentum continuation.

closed_positions.exit_reason: ~3.5% portfolio weight being freed. AKAM is the weakest position in the book — +1.1% unrealized gain, but negative analyst coverage ('3 reasons to avoid AKAM') published today, 36.9% 1Y rally means much of the revaluation is priced in, Fwd P/E 15.2x with only 7% revenue growth and high D/E (114x) is not compelling vs. alternatives. Capital can be redeployed into higher-conviction setups with better risk/reward. Freeing ~$3,475 to redeploy.

~2.2% portfolio weight being freed. DOCN has the weakest fundamental case in the book: 51.6x fwd P/E for 18% revenue growth with no near-term earnings catalyst (reports not in watchlist). Iran escalation reversal reduces risk appetite for speculative mid-cap cloud names. +4.9% gain is worth taking. Capital can be redeployed more efficiently. No sector overlap concern — freeing capital for better risk/reward.

~4.2% portfolio weight being freed. US lawmakers proposing crackdown on chip tool sales to China is a direct and material headwind to Applied Materials' revenue base — China is a significant % of AMAT revenue. Combined with -2% revenue growth (already decelerating), fwd P/E 25x is not compelling. This thesis is now compromised by a specific regulatory catalyst. Exiting with +2% gain before risk materializes. Semiconductor sector still represented by NVDA, MRVL, MU, TSM, ASML, ARM.

LNG is ~0.6% portfolio weight at 2 shares. Closing entire position: Iran cease-fire diplomacy (Trump olive branch) is directly undermining the war-premium LNG thesis that justified entry. Stock -4.3% 1W while benchmarks +3.9-5.8% — massive underperformance. The core thesis was Iran war escalation driving global LNG demand rerouting; if Iran de-escalates, this thesis is broken. Taking +1.0% gain before it turns negative. No sector overlap concern; energy broadly weak (CVX -5.8%, XOM -4.5%).

~1% portfolio weight being freed. Iran cease-fire is confirmed today (oil plunging, Dow +1,300) — this directly removes the primary catalyst for holding RTX: 'Iran war escalation creates near-term catalyst for defense contract acceleration.' Barrons headline: 'Defense Stocks Mixed After Iran Cease-Fire. Who's Winning and Who's Losing.' RTX fwd P/E 27x on 12% revenue growth with the war premium now gone is not compelling. Locking in +3.7% gain before thesis deteriorates further. No sector overlap concern — defense premium is evaporating across the board.

~0.6% portfolio weight being freed (small remaining position). TSEM at +18.5% unrealized gain, 42.5x fwd P/E on only 14% revenue growth — extremely stretched valuation after +76.4% 1M parabolic move. This was trimmed previously for same reasons; the full exit captures remaining profit before mean reversion. The stock is -2.5% from 52W high meaning very little upside room. AI foundry thesis is better expressed through TSM (larger position, better valuation at 20.4x fwd P/E on 20% revenue growth). Freeing capital for better risk/reward names.

1. Covering the full NOW short position (200 shares at ~$89.06 = ~$17,812 cost to cover). This was a ~12% portfolio weight in short exposure. 2. NOW short is only +0.8% profitable despite being a high-conviction thesis. Critical reasons to cover NOW: (a) Earnings April 22 — ONE WEEK away, creating massive binary risk; a ServiceNow beat or raised guidance could cause a 10-15% squeeze that would eliminate all profits and more. (b) Software sector is bouncing today — Morgan Stanley 'managed agents bullish for software' narrative gaining traction. (c) Nasdaq has rallied 9 consecutive days in risk-on regime — shorting momentum against the broader trend is dangerous near earnings. (d) Goldman's 'reality check on AI disruption will hang over for years' is bearish narrative but medium-term, not this week's trade. 3. Eliminates the most pressing near-term binary risk in the portfolio. ZS short (no imminent earnings) remains as our software short expression.

1. Represents ~1% portfolio weight (1 share at $1,481.77). 2. Earnings just reported TODAY and Yahoo Finance confirms 'ASML stock falls post-earnings' — the negative post-earnings reaction is the key signal to exit. Holding a 1-share position that has already captured +13.1% gain through a negative earnings reaction creates asymmetric downside risk with no near-term catalyst to recover. 3. Eliminates binary earnings overhang; capital freed adds to cash buffer ahead of TSM earnings tomorrow. No sector overlap concern — semiconductor exposure well maintained through NVDA, AVGO, MU, AMD, MRVL, TSM.
